([Dutch](/wiki/Dutch_language "Dutch language"): *Arrondissement Gent*; [French](/wiki/French_language "French language"): *Arrondissement de Gand*) is the largest of the six administrative [arrondissements](/wiki/Arrondissements_of_Belgium "Arrondissements of Belgium") in the [Province](/wiki/Provinces_of_Belgium "Provinces of Belgium") of [East Flanders](/wiki/East_Flanders "East Flanders"), [Belgium](/wiki/Belgium "Belgium").[[1]](#cite_note-1) It is both an [administrative](/wiki/Arrondissements_of_Belgium#Administrative "Arrondissements of Belgium") and a [judicial arrondissement](/wiki/Arrondissements_of_Belgium#Judicial "Arrondissements of Belgium"). However, the Judicial Arrondissement of Ghent also comprises the municipalities of the [Arrondissement of Eeklo](/wiki/Arrondissement_of_Eeklo "Arrondissement of Eeklo").

["Flandre-Orientale en néerlandais Oost-Vlaanderen"](https://www.larousse.fr/encyclopedie/autre-region/Flandre-Orientale/119623) [East Flanders and Dutch East Flanders]. *www.larousse.fr* (in French). Retrieved 25 June 2025.

The **Arrondissement of Ghent** ([Dutch](/wiki/Dutch_language "Dutch language"): *Arrondissement Gent*; [French](/wiki/French_language "French language"): *Arrondissement de Gand*) is the largest of the six administrative [arrondissements](/wiki/Arrondissements_of_Belgium "Arrondissements of Belgium") in the [Province](/wiki/Provinces_of_Belgium "Provinces of Belgium") of [East Flanders](/wiki/East_Flanders "East Flanders"), [Belgium](/wiki/Belgium "Belgium").[[1]](#cite_note-1) It is both an [administrative](/wiki/Arrondissements_of_Belgium#Administrative "Arrondissements of Belgium") and a [judicial arrondissement](/wiki/Arrondissements_of_Belgium#Judicial "Arrondissements of Belgium"). However, the Judicial Arrondissement of Ghent also comprises the municipalities of the [Arrondissement of Eeklo](/wiki/Arrondissement_of_Eeklo "Arrondissement of Eeklo").

## History

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Arrondissement_of_Ghent&action=edit&section=1 "Edit section: History")]

The Arrondissement of Ghent was created in 1800 as the first arrondissement in the [Department](/wiki/130_departments_of_the_First_French_Empire "130 departments of the First French Empire") of [Escaut](/wiki/Escaut_(department) "Escaut (department)") ([Dutch](/wiki/Dutch_language "Dutch language"): *Departement Schelde*). It originally comprised the cantons of [Deinze](/wiki/Deinze "Deinze"), [Eeklo](/wiki/Eeklo "Eeklo"), [Evergem](/wiki/Evergem "Evergem"), [Ghent](/wiki/Ghent "Ghent"), [Kruishoutem](/wiki/Kruishoutem "Kruishoutem"), [Lochristi](/wiki/Lochristi "Lochristi"), [Nazareth](/wiki/Nazareth,_Belgium "Nazareth, Belgium"), [Nevele](/wiki/Nevele "Nevele"), [Oosterzele](/wiki/Oosterzele "Oosterzele"), [Waarschoot](/wiki/Waarschoot "Waarschoot") and [Zomergem](/wiki/Zomergem "Zomergem"). In 1803, the canton of Eeklo was merged with the Arrondissement of Sas-van-Gent to form the [Arrondissement of Eeklo](/wiki/Arrondissement_of_Eeklo "Arrondissement of Eeklo"). The canton of Kruishoutem was ceded to the [Arrondissement of Oudenaarde](/wiki/Arrondissement_of_Oudenaarde "Arrondissement of Oudenaarde") in 1818.

In 1921, parts of Laarne and Kalken (in the [Arrondissement of Dendermonde](/wiki/Arrondissement_of_Dendermonde "Arrondissement of Dendermonde")) were added to the arrondissement to form the new municipality of Beervelde. Parts of [Kluizen](/wiki/Kluizen "Kluizen"), [Ertvelde](/wiki/Ertvelde "Ertvelde") en [Zelzate](/wiki/Zelzate "Zelzate") were added to the arrondissement in 1927 from the [Arrondissement of Eeklo](/wiki/Arrondissement_of_Eeklo "Arrondissement of Eeklo"). In 1977, the municipality of Ertvelde (of which Kluizen had been a part since 1965) in the Arrondissement of Eeklo ceased to exist and was merged into [Evergem](/wiki/Evergem "Evergem"). As a result, it was added to the Arrondissement of Ghent.

Per 1 January 2019, the municipalities of [Aalter](/wiki/Aalter "Aalter") and [Knesselare](/wiki/Knesselare "Knesselare") were merged into Aalter, [Deinze](/wiki/Deinze "Deinze") and [Nevele](/wiki/Nevele "Nevele") into Deinze, and [Waarschoot](/wiki/Waarschoot "Waarschoot"), [Lovendegem](/wiki/Lovendegem "Lovendegem") and [Zomergem](/wiki/Zomergem "Zomergem") into [Lievegem](/wiki/Lievegem "Lievegem"). The number of municipalities in this arrondissement was thus be reduced from 21 to 17.

## Municipalities

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Arrondissement_of_Ghent&action=edit&section=2 "Edit section: Municipalities")]

The Administrative Arrondissement of Ghent consists of the following [municipalities](/wiki/Municipalities_of_Belgium "Municipalities of Belgium"):

|  |  |  |
| --- | --- | --- |
| * [Aalter](/wiki/Aalter "Aalter") * [Deinze](/wiki/Deinze "Deinze") * [Destelbergen](/wiki/Destelbergen "Destelbergen") * [Evergem](/wiki/Evergem "Evergem") * [Gavere](/wiki/Gavere "Gavere") | * [Ghent](/wiki/Ghent "Ghent") * [Lievegem](/wiki/Lievegem "Lievegem") * [Lochristi](/wiki/Lochristi "Lochristi") * [Merelbeke-Melle](/wiki/Merelbeke-Melle "Merelbeke-Melle") | * [Nazareth-De Pinte](/wiki/Nazareth-De_Pinte "Nazareth-De Pinte") * [Oosterzele](/wiki/Oosterzele "Oosterzele") * [Sint-Martens-Latem](/wiki/Sint-Martens-Latem "Sint-Martens-Latem") * [Zulte](/wiki/Zulte "Zulte") |
